ddls:
CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was collected */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site where the sample was taken */,
  "sample_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of the sample (e.g., soil, rock) */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who collected the sample */,
  "sampling_method" TEXT /* Method used for sampling (e.g., drilling, grab sampling) */,
  "sample_description" TEXT /* Description of the sample */,
  "sample_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the sample (e.g., good, poor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_sample_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_type_id") REFERENCES sample_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographical location of the site */,
  "site_description" TEXT /* Description of the geological characteristics of the site */,
  "geological_formation" TEXT /* Geological formation of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_name" TEXT /* Full name of the operator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the operator */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation or organization of the operator */,
  "operator_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the operator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "elemental_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample being analyzed */,
  "analysis_method_id" INTEGER /* Method used for analysis */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was performed */,
  "ri" REAL /* RI value of the sample */,
  "na" REAL /* Sodium concentration in the sample */,
  "mg" REAL /* Magnesium concentration in the sample */,
  "al" REAL /* Aluminum concentration in the sample */,
  "si" REAL /* Silicon concentration in the sample */,
  "ka" REAL /* Potassium concentration in the sample */,
  "ca" REAL /* Calcium concentration in the sample */,
  "ba" REAL /* Barium concentration in the sample */,
  "fe" REAL /* Iron concentration in the sample */,
  "analysis_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_elemental_analysis_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_elemental_analysis_analysis_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("analysis_method_id") REFERENCES analysis_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sample type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated with the note */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text content of the note */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the note was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sample_notes_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sample_notes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site associated with the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the site */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status update */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated with the status */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sample (e.g., collected, under analysis, completed) */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the status was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sample_status_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the method used for analysis */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the analysis method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample being collaborated on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user involved in the collaboration */,
  "collaboration_role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the collaboration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sample_collaborations_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sample_collaborations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user with the permission */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated with the permission */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)
